epicReference.title: Core Task Platform
epicReference.goal: Build the foundational task management platform with authentication, task CRUD, and project organization

keyAchievements: Authentication system passed security audit with zero critical findings
Task CRUD API performance far exceeded targets (87ms p95 vs 200ms target)
Clean architecture allows easy extension for upcoming AI integration
Design system established and documented in Storybook for team consistency
mainChallenges: Concurrent task reordering (drag-and-drop) required 2 iterations to get right
CI/CD Docker Hub rate limiting caused delays in Sprints 1 and 2
Initial database schema needed revision after discovering label management requirements

velocityAnalysis.varianceReason: S-1.2 drag-and-drop concurrent handling was more complex than estimated, adding ~5 points

whatWentWell.item: TypeScript strict mode enforced from day one
whatWentWell.impact: Caught 23 potential runtime errors at compile time during the epic
whatWentWell.recommendation: Continue strict mode for all future epics; it pays for itself quickly

whatWentWell.item: Zod schema validation on all API inputs
whatWentWell.impact: Zero input validation bugs in production. Security audit specifically praised this approach.
whatWentWell.recommendation: Expand to output validation for critical endpoints in EPIC-2

whatWentWell.item: Pair programming on complex drag-and-drop logic
whatWentWell.impact: Second iteration solved the concurrent reordering problem in 2 days vs estimated 5
whatWentWell.recommendation: Use pair programming for algorithmically complex features going forward

whatDidNotGoWell.impact: Lost 1.5 days on migration and data transformation for the labels feature
whatDidNotGoWell.rootCause: Labels requirement was underspecified in story - discovered multi-project labels needed a join table
whatDidNotGoWell.recommendation: Require database schema review as part of story grooming for data-model changes

whatDidNotGoWell.item: Docker Hub rate limiting in CI/CD
whatDidNotGoWell.impact: Caused ~4 hours of cumulative delays over 2 sprints from failed builds and retries
whatDidNotGoWell.rootCause: Using Docker Hub free tier for base images without caching layer
whatDidNotGoWell.recommendation: Migrate to ECR image caching (already in progress, scheduled for completion March 18)

whatDidNotGoWell.item: E2E tests for OAuth flows were written too late
whatDidNotGoWell.impact: OAuth error handling bugs found in week 4 instead of week 2
whatDidNotGoWell.rootCause: E2E test writing was deferred to the end of the story instead of written alongside feature code
whatDidNotGoWell.recommendation: Write E2E tests concurrently with feature implementation, not after

lessonsLearned.lesson: Database schema changes should be reviewed during story grooming, not discovered during implementation
appliesTo: Any story involving new data entities

lessonsLearned.lesson: Concurrent data modification (like drag-and-drop reordering) always takes 2x the initial estimate
Investing in design system components upfront accelerated later stories significantly

storyAnalysis.notes: Well-specified story with clear acceptance criteria. Security audit pass on first attempt.
Core CRUD was straightforward but concurrent drag-and-drop reordering required significant rework.
blockers: Concurrent position update race condition required architectural rethink

storyAnalysis.notes: Required mid-sprint database schema migration for multi-project labels. Feature works well after rework.

debtIntroduced.description: Task position column uses integer stepping (position = 1000, 2000, ...) which can exhaust gaps after many reorders
debtIntroduced.reason: Simpler to implement than fractional indexing for MVP; works well for typical usage patterns
debtIntroduced.remediationPlan: Implement fractional indexing or periodic rebalancing in EPIC-3 or post-launch

debtIntroduced.description: OAuth token refresh logic has a 500ms retry hardcoded instead of exponential backoff
debtIntroduced.reason: Time pressure to complete S-1.1 on schedule
debtIntroduced.remediationPlan: Refactor to exponential backoff before EPIC-4 integration work begins

debtAddressed.description: Initial prototype used raw SQL queries
debtAddressed.resolution: Migrated all queries to Drizzle ORM with parameterized queries during S-1.2

nextEpicImpacts.impact: Task data model and API endpoints are stable and well-tested, providing a solid foundation for AI priority scoring integration
nextEpicImpacts.recommendation: S-2.1 can proceed as planned; leverage existing task query endpoints for AI model input

nextEpicImpacts.impact: TaskCard component and design system are ready for Focus Mode integration
nextEpicImpacts.recommendation: Reuse existing TaskCard variants; add new 'focus-mode' variant rather than building separate components

architectureChanges.decision: Adopted optimistic updates pattern for task mutations
architectureChanges.rationale: Provides instant UI feedback for task operations; rollback on server error. Discovered during S-1.2 performance optimization.
architectureChanges.documentationUpdate: Update architecture doc ADR section with optimistic update pattern guidelines

newDiscoveries.discovery: Real-time WebSocket for task sync adds significant complexity without clear user demand
newDiscoveries.implication: Polling with 5-second intervals provides acceptable UX for single-user scenarios; WebSocket can be deferred
newDiscoveries.action: Remove WebSocket requirement from S-1.2; add as optional enhancement in EPIC-4 if team collaboration features are prioritized

suggestedBacklogChanges.description: Add OAuth token refresh improvement as a prerequisite task before building integration connectors
suggestedBacklogChanges.rationale: Current hardcoded retry logic will cause issues with multiple concurrent integration syncs

processImprovements: Add database schema review to story grooming checklist
Write E2E tests alongside features, not after
Consider pair programming for complex algorithmic work
toolingImprovements: Migrate CI/CD to ECR image caching to eliminate Docker Hub rate limiting
Add Storybook visual regression testing for design system components
communicationImprovements: Daily standup async updates in Slack are working well - continue
Sprint review demos should include live API performance metrics dashboard

actionItems.action: Add database schema review to story grooming template
Complete ECR image caching migration for CI/CD
Refactor OAuth token refresh to exponential backoff
Update architecture documentation with optimistic update pattern ADR
